body_txt: An older character who is still perfectly capable is suddenly faced with mandatory retirement, is despondent at being forced to give up a career and income they enjoy and is made to feel useless. The other characters resolve to help the potential retiree and the plot usually concludes with the management cancelling the relevant policy and allow the older character to stay on board for as long as they are capable. In an even dirtier twist on the trope the retirement is forced not because of a general policy, but for political reasons. Most frequently, it is because the retiree is sitting on a position that is coveted by an ambitious younger candidate who is not as skilled as them but has better personal connections . Or it is because they have a Reasonable Authority Figure whose belief in the Good Old Ways makes them an obstacle for the new tyrant in charge . You may have an Elder Employee on your hands if the efforts to stay are successful. For the opposite of this trope, see Mandatory Unretirement . Compare and contrast Empty Nest . Likely to suffer from Job Mindset Inertia .
